mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals strong labor market dynamics that boost corporate earnings visibility, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in triggered periods show negative or neutral correlation (Rank IC < 0.05) with the joint PAYEMS/UNRATE signal.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 225 in CPIAUCSL indicates central bank inaction on price pressures, prompting equity investors to demand higher risk premiums and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in CPIAUCSL > 225 periods exceed 1.5% in more than 40% of cases despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Rising residential construction (HOUST) combined with strong consumer spending (RSAFS) indicates robust demand-side momentum, driving positive S&P 500 returns during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in triggered periods show negative correlation (Rank IC < -0.1) with the HOUST/RSAFS interaction.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and GDP (GDPC1) signals expanding supply-side capacity, which reduces inflationary pressures and supports positive equity returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in INDPRO/GDPC1 growth periods show negative or neutral correlation (Rank IC < 0.05) with the signal.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4% signals aggressive monetary tightening, increasing borrowing costs and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in FEDFUNDS > 4% periods exceed 1.5% in more than 40% of cases despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) indicate elevated consumer confidence and demand for durable goods, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in HOUST > 500 periods show negative or neutral correlation (Rank IC < 0.05) with the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects improved manufacturing activity, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in INDPRO growth periods show negative correlation (Rank IC < -0.1) with the signal.
